package extensions // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/layout/extensions"

Package extensions provides the framework for registering and applying
extensions to the scaffold generation process.

VARIABLES

var Files embed.FS
    Files contains the templates provided by scaffold extensions.


FUNCTIONS

func Names() []string
    Names returns all registered extension names in sorted order.

func Register(ext Extension) error
    Register adds an extension to the global registry.


TYPES

type AwsSes struct{}
    AwsSes adds AWS SES email client support to a scaffolded project.

func (e AwsSes) Apply(ctx *Context) error
    Apply adds AWS SES configuration, providers, and client files.

func (e AwsSes) Dependencies() []string
    Dependencies returns extension names that must be applied first.

func (e AwsSes) Name() string
    Name returns the extension name used in lock files and CLI flags.

type Context struct {
	TargetDir         string
	Data              TemplateData
	ProcessTemplate   ProcessTemplateFunc
	AddPostStep       func(func(targetDir string) error)
	NextMigrationTime *time.Time
	Inertia           string // inertia adapter, e.g. "vue", "react", "svelte"
}
    Context carries state and callbacks for applying an extension.

func (ctx *Context) Builder() *blueprint.Builder
    Builder returns the blueprint builder for structured contributions.

type CssComponents struct{}
    CssComponents adds optional CSS component examples to a scaffolded project.

func (c CssComponents) Apply(ctx *Context) error
    Apply renders component CSS and example templates.

func (c CssComponents) Dependencies() []string
    Dependencies returns extension names that must be applied first.

func (c CssComponents) Name() string
    Name returns the extension name used in lock files and CLI flags.

type Docker struct{}
    Docker adds Docker build files to a scaffolded project.

func (d Docker) Apply(ctx *Context) error
    Apply renders Docker templates into the target project.

func (d Docker) Dependencies() []string
    Dependencies returns extension names that must be applied first.

func (d Docker) Name() string
    Name returns the extension name used in lock files and CLI flags.

type Extension interface {
	Name() string
	Apply(ctx *Context) error
	Dependencies() []string
}
    Extension adds files, blueprint entries, or post-processing steps to a
    scaffold.

func Get(name string) (Extension, bool)
    Get returns a registered extension by name.

type ProcessTemplateFunc func(templateFile, targetPath string, data TemplateData) error
    ProcessTemplateFunc renders an extension template into a target file.

type TemplateData interface {
	DatabaseDialect() string
	GetModuleName() string
	GetInertia() string
	Builder() *blueprint.Builder
	SetBlueprint(bp *blueprint.Blueprint)
}
    TemplateData exposes scaffold data to extension templates and blueprints.


## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/layout/templates
package templates // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/layout/templates"

Package templates exposes the embedded templates used to scaffold Andurel
projects.

VARIABLES

var Files embed.FS
    Files contains the templates used to scaffold Andurel projects.


## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/layout/upgrade
package upgrade // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/layout/upgrade"

Package upgrade plans and applies transactional upgrades to generated projects.

TYPES

type FileDiff struct {
	Path string `json:"path"`
	Diff string `json:"diff"`
}
    FileDiff is a deterministic unified diff for one planned path.

type FrameworkTemplate struct {
	TemplateName string
	TargetPath   string
}
    FrameworkTemplate represents a framework element template and its target
    path

func GetFrameworkTemplates(config *layout.ScaffoldConfig) []FrameworkTemplate
    GetFrameworkTemplates returns the list of framework element templates These
    are the only files that get upgraded when running andurel upgrade

type GitAnalyzer struct {
	// Has unexported fields.
}
    GitAnalyzer represents git analyzer.

func NewGitAnalyzer(projectRoot string) *GitAnalyzer
    NewGitAnalyzer creates a new git analyzer.

func (g *GitAnalyzer) GetFileFromInitialCommit(relPath string) ([]byte, error)
    GetFileFromInitialCommit returns file from initial commit.

func (g *GitAnalyzer) GetModifiedFiles() (map[string]bool, error)
    GetModifiedFiles returns modified files.

func (g *GitAnalyzer) IsClean() (bool, error)
    IsClean reports whether clean.

type TemplateGenerator struct {
	// Has unexported fields.
}
    TemplateGenerator generates template artifacts.

func NewTemplateGenerator(targetVersion string) *TemplateGenerator
    NewTemplateGenerator creates a new template generator.

func (g *TemplateGenerator) RenderFrameworkTemplates(
	projectRoot string,
	config layout.ScaffoldConfig,
	extensions []string,
) (map[string][]byte, error)
    RenderFrameworkTemplates renders all framework element templates and returns
    a map of file paths to their rendered content

type ToolSyncResult struct {
	Added    []string
	Removed  []string
	Updated  []string
	Metadata []string
}
    ToolSyncResult represents the result of synchronizing tools

type UpgradeOptions struct {
	DryRun        bool
	Auto          bool
	Repair        bool
	TargetVersion string
}
    UpgradeOptions configures upgrade behavior.

type UpgradeReport struct {
	FromVersion   string
	ToVersion     string
	FilesReplaced int
	FilesRemoved  int

	ToolsAdded   int
	ToolsRemoved int
	ToolsUpdated int

	AddedTools          []string
	RemovedTools        []string
	UpdatedTools        []string
	ToolMetadataChanges []string
	ReplacedFiles       []string
	RemovedFiles        []string
	Diffs               []FileDiff
	DirtyWorktree       bool
	AlreadyCurrent      bool
	RepairAvailable     bool
	RepairApplied       bool

	Success bool
	Error   error
}
    UpgradeReport represents upgrade report.

type Upgrader struct {
	// Has unexported fields.
}
    Upgrader represents upgrader.

func NewUpgrader(projectRoot string, opts UpgradeOptions) (*Upgrader, error)
    NewUpgrader creates a new upgrader.

func (u *Upgrader) Execute() (*UpgradeReport, error)
    Execute performs the execute operation.


## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/layout/versions
package versions // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/layout/versions"

Package versions defines default versions for tools installed in generated
projects.

CONSTANTS

const (
	// Templ is the default templ tool version.
	Templ = "v0.3.1020"
	// Goose is the default goose migration tool version.
	Goose = "v3.27.1"
	// Mailpit is the default Mailpit tool version.
	Mailpit = "v1.30.2"
	// Usql is the default usql tool version.
	Usql = "v0.21.4"
	// Dblab is the default dblab tool version.
	Dblab = "v0.40.2"
	// TailwindCLI is the default Tailwind CSS CLI version.
	TailwindCLI = "v4.3.2"
	// Shadowfax is the default Shadowfax runner version.
	Shadowfax = "v0.8.4"
)

## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/cache
package cache // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/cache"

Package cache provides TTL caching for repeated filesystem discovery operations.

FUNCTIONS

func CleanupExpiredFileSystemEntries()
    CleanupExpiredFileSystemEntries cleans up expired file system entries.

func ClearFileSystemCache()
    ClearFileSystemCache clears file system cache.

func GetDirectoryRoot(key string, resolver func() (string, error)) (string, error)
    GetDirectoryRoot returns a cached directory root or resolves and caches it.

func GetFileExists(key string, checker func() bool) bool
    GetFileExists returns a cached file existence check or runs and caches it.

func GetModulePath(key string, resolver func() (string, error)) (string, error)
    GetModulePath returns a cached module path or resolves and caches it.


TYPES

type Entry struct {
	Value     any
	ExpiresAt time.Time
}
    Entry stores a cached value and its expiration time.

func (e *Entry) IsExpired() bool
    IsExpired reports whether the entry is past its expiration time.

type FileSystemCache struct {
	// Has unexported fields.
}
    FileSystemCache is a small TTL cache for repeated filesystem lookups.

func NewFileSystemCache(ttl time.Duration) *FileSystemCache
    NewFileSystemCache creates a new file system cache.

func (fsc *FileSystemCache) CleanupExpired()
    CleanupExpired removes entries whose TTL has elapsed.

func (fsc *FileSystemCache) Clear()
    Clear removes all cached entries.

func (fsc *FileSystemCache) Delete(key string)
    Delete removes a value from the cache.

func (fsc *FileSystemCache) Get(key string) (any, bool)
    Get returns a cached value when it exists and has not expired.

func (fsc *FileSystemCache) Set(key string, value any)
    Set stores a value under key using the cache TTL.


## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/constants
package constants // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/constants"

Package constants defines shared filesystem permissions used by Andurel.

CONSTANTS

const (
	// FilePermissionPrivate is used for files containing private data.
	FilePermissionPrivate os.FileMode = 0o600

	// FilePermissionPublic is used for regular readable project files.
	FilePermissionPublic os.FileMode = 0o644

	// DirPermissionDefault is used for regular project directories.
	DirPermissionDefault os.FileMode = 0o755

	// DirPermissionPrivate is used for directories containing private data.
	DirPermissionPrivate os.FileMode = 0o700
)

## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/errors
package errors // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/errors"

Package errors provides contextual and generator-specific error types.

FUNCTIONS

func IsRecoverable(err error) bool
    IsRecoverable checks if an error is recoverable

func NewContextualError(operation, resource, file string, cause error) error
    NewContextualError creates a new contextual error

func NewDatabaseError(operation, table string, err error) error
    NewDatabaseError creates a new database error.

func NewFileOperationError(path, operation string, err error) error
    NewFileOperationError wraps an error that occurred while operating on a
    file.

func NewGeneratorError(operation, resource string, err error) error
    NewGeneratorError wraps an error that occurred while generating a resource.

func NewSpecificFileOperationError(path, operation string, err error) error
    NewSpecificFileOperationError wraps an error with file path and operation
    context.

func NewTemplateError(templateName, operation string, err error) error
    NewTemplateError wraps an error from template processing.

func NewValidationError(field, value, reason string, cause error) error
    NewValidationError creates a new validation error.

func WrapDatabaseError(err error, operation, table string) error
    WrapDatabaseError wraps a database error

func WrapError(err error, ctx ErrorContext) error
    WrapError wraps an error with context

func WrapErrorWithCaller(err error, ctx ErrorContext) error
    WrapErrorWithCaller wraps an error with context and caller information

func WrapFileError(err error, operation, filePath string) error
    WrapFileError wraps a file operation error

func WrapGenerationError(err error, operation, resource string) error
    WrapGenerationError wraps a generation error

func WrapTemplateError(err error, operation, templateName string) error
    WrapTemplateError wraps a template error

func WrapValidationError(err error, field, value string) error
    WrapValidationError wraps a validation error


TYPES

type ContextualError struct {
	Context *ErrorContext `json:"context"`
	Cause   error         `json:"cause"`
}
    ContextualError provides enhanced error information with context

func (e *ContextualError) Error() string
    Error implements the error interface

func (e *ContextualError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap returns the underlying cause

type DatabaseError struct {
	Operation string
	Table     string
	Cause     error
}
    DatabaseError adds database operation and table context to an error.

func (e *DatabaseError) Error() string
    Error returns the database failure message.

func (e *DatabaseError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap returns the underlying cause.

type DefaultErrorRecovery struct{}
    DefaultErrorRecovery provides basic error recovery strategies

func (der *DefaultErrorRecovery) CanRecover(err error) bool
    CanRecover determines if an error is recoverable

func (der *DefaultErrorRecovery) Recover(err error) error
    Recover attempts to recover from an error

type ErrorBuilder struct {
	// Has unexported fields.
}
    ErrorBuilder provides a fluent interface for building contextual errors

func NewErrorBuilder() *ErrorBuilder
    NewErrorBuilder creates a new error builder

func (eb *ErrorBuilder) Detail(key string, value any) *ErrorBuilder
    Detail adds a detail

func (eb *ErrorBuilder) File(file string) *ErrorBuilder
    File sets the file

func (eb *ErrorBuilder) New(message string) error
    New creates a new error with the built context

func (eb *ErrorBuilder) Operation(operation string) *ErrorBuilder
    Operation sets the operation

func (eb *ErrorBuilder) Resource(resource string) *ErrorBuilder
    Resource sets the resource

func (eb *ErrorBuilder) Wrap(err error) error
    Wrap wraps an error with the built context

type ErrorContext struct {
	Operation string         `json:"operation"`
	Resource  string         `json:"resource"`
	File      string         `json:"file"`
	Details   map[string]any `json:"details"`
}
    ErrorContext provides context for error wrapping

func NewErrorContext(operation, resource, file string) *ErrorContext
    NewErrorContext creates a new error context

func (ec *ErrorContext) WithCaller(skip int) *ErrorContext
    WithCaller adds caller information to the error context

func (ec *ErrorContext) WithDetail(key string, value any) *ErrorContext
    WithDetail adds a detail to the error context

type ErrorRecovery interface {
	Recover(err error) error
	CanRecover(err error) bool
}
    ErrorRecovery defines strategies for error recovery

type FileOperationError struct {
	Path      string
	Operation string
	Cause     error
}
    FileOperationError adds file path and operation context to an error.

func (e *FileOperationError) Error() string
    Error returns the file operation failure message.

func (e *FileOperationError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap returns the underlying cause.

type GenerationError struct {
	Operation string
	Resource  string
	File      string
	Cause     error
}
    GenerationError adds generation context to an underlying error.

func (e *GenerationError) Error() string
    Error returns the generation failure message.

func (e *GenerationError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap returns the underlying cause.

type TemplateError struct {
	TemplateName string
	Operation    string
	Cause        error
}
    TemplateError adds template name and operation context to an error.

func (e *TemplateError) Error() string
    Error returns the template failure message.

func (e *TemplateError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap returns the underlying cause.

type ValidationError struct {
	Field  string
	Value  string
	Reason string
	Cause  error
}
    ValidationError adds field, value, and reason context to a validation error.

func (e *ValidationError) Error() string
    Error returns the validation failure message.

func (e *ValidationError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap returns the underlying cause.


## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/naming
package naming // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/naming"

Package naming transforms resource, identifier, and database names.

FUNCTIONS

func Capitalize(s string) string
    Capitalize performs capitalize.

func ControllerPackageName(namespace string) string
    ControllerPackageName returns the package name for a controller file given
    an optional namespace. Empty namespace yields "controllers".

func DeriveResourceName(tableName string) string
    DeriveResourceName converts a snake_case plural table name into a PascalCase
    singular resource name. Examples: "user_roles" -> "UserRole", "products" ->
    "Product", "admin_users" -> "AdminUser"

func DeriveTableName(resourceName string) string
    DeriveTableName converts a CamelCase resource name into a snake_case plural
    table name.

func IsValidNamespace(namespace string) bool
    IsValidNamespace validates that a namespace is a valid Go package name and
    not a reserved path.

func NamespaceFilePrefix(namespace string) string
    NamespaceFilePrefix converts a slash-separated namespace path into the file
    prefix used for same-package generated artifacts such as route and view
    files.

func NamespaceFromResource(resourceName string) (namespace, name string)
    NamespaceFromResource splits a namespaced resource name into its namespace
    and resource name parts. Invalid namespaced input is returned as an
    unnamespaced resource for backward-compatible callers that cannot surface an
    error; command paths should use ParseNamespacedResource.

func NamespaceRouteName(namespace string) string
    NamespaceRouteName converts a slash-separated namespace path into a dotted
    route-name prefix.

func NamespaceToPascal(namespace string) string
    NamespaceToPascal converts a slash-separated namespace path into a
    PascalCase prefix suitable for route variables and generated view symbols.

func NamespacedControllerImportPath(modulePath, namespace string) string
    NamespacedControllerImportPath returns the import path for a controller
    package, optionally namespaced.

func ParseNamespacedResource(input string) (namespace, resource string, err error)
    ParseNamespacedResource splits a resource name into an optional single-level
    namespace and resource name. Namespaces are intentionally lower-case Go
    package names; resource names remain the original PascalCase generator
    input.

func ToCamelCase(s string) string
    ToCamelCase converts a snake_case identifier into camelCase. Examples:
    "admin_users" -> "adminUsers", "product_categories" -> "productCategories"

func ToKebabCase(s string) string
    ToKebabCase converts a snake_case identifier into kebab-case.

func ToLowerCamelCase(s string) string
    ToLowerCamelCase converts a PascalCase identifier into camelCase by
    lowercasing the first character. Examples: "NewUser" -> "newUser",
    "AdminUser" -> "adminUser", "User" -> "user"

func ToLowerCamelCaseFromAny(s string) string
    ToLowerCamelCaseFromAny converts snake_case or PascalCase into lower
    camelCase. It preserves existing camelCase inputs by only lowercasing the
    first character when there are no underscores.

func ToPascalCase(s string) string
    ToPascalCase converts a snake_case identifier into PascalCase. Examples:
    "admin_users" -> "AdminUsers", "product_categories" -> "ProductCategories"

func ToReceiverName(s string) string
    ToReceiverName generates a short receiver name from a PascalCase
    identifier by extracting and lowercasing all uppercase letters. Examples:
    "StudentFeedback" -> "sf", "Product" -> "p", "UserRole" -> "ur"

func ToSnakeCase(s string) string
    ToSnakeCase converts a CamelCase identifier into snake_case.


## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/testing
package testing // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/pkg/testing"

Package testing provides helpers for organizing unit, integration, and
end-to-end tests.

TYPES

type EndToEndTest struct {
	Name        string
	Description string
	Setup       func() error
	Teardown    func() error
	TestFunc    func(t *testing.T)
}
    EndToEndTest represents an end-to-end test

type IntegrationTest struct {
	Name        string
	Description string
	Setup       func() error
	Teardown    func() error
	TestFunc    func(t *testing.T)
}
    IntegrationTest represents an integration test

type TableDrivenTest struct {
	Name     string
	Tests    []TestData
	TestFunc func(t *testing.T, test TestData)
}
    TableDrivenTest provides a consistent pattern for table-driven tests

func NewTableDrivenTest(
	name string,
	tests []TestData,
	testFunc func(t *testing.T, test TestData),
) *TableDrivenTest
    NewTableDrivenTest creates a new table-driven test

func (tdt *TableDrivenTest) Run(t *testing.T)
    Run executes the table-driven test

type TestData struct {
	Name     string
	Input    any
	Expected any
	Error    error
}
    TestData represents a generic test case structure

type TestSuite struct {
	Unit        map[string]UnitTest
	Integration map[string]IntegrationTest
	E2E         map[string]EndToEndTest
}
    TestSuite organizes tests by functionality

func NewTestSuite() *TestSuite
    NewTestSuite creates a new test suite

func (ts *TestSuite) AddE2ETest(
	name, description string,
	setup, teardown func() error,
	testFunc func(t *testing.T),
)
    AddE2ETest adds an end-to-end test to the suite

func (ts *TestSuite) AddIntegrationTest(
	name, description string,
	setup, teardown func() error,
	testFunc func(t *testing.T),
)
    AddIntegrationTest adds an integration test to the suite

func (ts *TestSuite) AddUnitTest(name, description string, testFunc func(t *testing.T))
    AddUnitTest adds a unit test to the suite

func (ts *TestSuite) RunAllTests(t *testing.T)
    RunAllTests runs all tests in the suite

func (ts *TestSuite) RunE2ETests(t *testing.T)
    RunE2ETests runs all end-to-end tests in the suite

func (ts *TestSuite) RunIntegrationTests(t *testing.T)
    RunIntegrationTests runs all integration tests in the suite

func (ts *TestSuite) RunUnitTests(t *testing.T)
    RunUnitTests runs all unit tests in the suite

type UnitTest struct {
	Name        string
	Description string
	TestFunc    func(t *testing.T)
}
    UnitTest represents a unit test


## github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/skills
package skills // import "github.com/mbvlabs/andurel/skills"

Package skills exposes the embedded Andurel skill documentation and assets.

VARIABLES

var AndurelSkill string
    AndurelSkill contains the embedded Andurel skill instructions.

var AndurelSkillFS embed.FS
    AndurelSkillFS contains the embedded Andurel skill files.


FUNCTIONS

func WalkAndurelSkillFiles(fn func(path string, data []byte) error) error
    WalkAndurelSkillFiles walks andurel skill files.
